questions.question: Define a triangle and give one example.
questions.answer: A triangle is a closed figure formed by three line segments joining three non-collinear points. Example: an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: State the angle sum property of a triangle.
questions.answer: The sum of the three interior angles of a triangle is 180°.

questions.question: Classify triangles based on sides.
questions.answer: Equilateral: all sides equal; Isosceles: two sides equal; Scalene: all sides unequal.

questions.question: Classify triangles based on angles.
questions.answer: Acute-angled: all angles < 90°; Right-angled: one angle = 90°; Obtuse-angled: one angle > 90°.

questions.question: Explain the concept of median of a triangle.
questions.answer: A median is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.question: Explain the concept of altitude of a triangle.
questions.answer: An altitude is a perpendicular drawn from a vertex to the opposite side or its extension.

questions.question: Explain the concept of angle bisector of a triangle.
questions.answer: An angle bisector divides the angle at a vertex into two equal angles.

questions.question: Explain the concept of perpendicular bisector of a side of a triangle.
questions.answer: A perpendicular bisector is a line perpendicular to a side and passing through its midpoint.

questions.question: State the triangle inequality theorem.
questions.answer: The sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.

questions.question: Explain the centroid of a triangle.
questions.answer: The centroid is the point of intersection of medians and divides each median in a 2:1 ratio.

questions.question: Explain the incenter of a triangle.
questions.answer: The incenter is the point of intersection of angle bisectors and is the center of the inscribed circle.

questions.question: Explain the circumcenter of a triangle.
questions.answer: The circumcenter is the intersection of perpendicular bisectors of sides and is the center of the circumscribed circle.

questions.question: Explain the orthocenter of a triangle.
questions.answer: The orthocenter is the intersection point of altitudes of a triangle.

questions.question: Explain the area of a triangle using base and height.
questions.answer: Area = 1/2 × base × height.

questions.question: Explain area of a triangle using Heron's formula.
questions.answer: Area = √[s(s−a)(s−b)(s−c)] where s = (a+b+c)/2 and a, b, c are sides of the triangle.

questions.question: Explain similarity of triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal and corresponding sides are proportional.

questions.question: State the criteria for similarity of triangles.
questions.answer: AA, SAS, and SSS are the criteria for similarity of triangles.

questions.question: Explain Pythagoras theorem in right-angled triangles.
questions.answer: In a right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

questions.question: Explain Pythagoras theorem using similar triangles.
questions.answer: By drawing a perpendicular from the right angle to the hypotenuse, the resulting smaller triangles are similar; using proportionality, we derive a² + b² = c².

questions.question: Explain properties of equilateral triangles.
questions.answer: All sides are equal, all angles are 60°, medians, altitudes, and angle bisectors coincide.

questions.question: Explain properties of isosceles triangles.
questions.answer: Two sides are equal, angles opposite equal sides are equal, medians and altitudes have special relations.

questions.question: Explain properties of scalene triangles.
questions.answer: All sides unequal, all angles unequal, medians, altitudes, and bisectors are distinct.

questions.question: Explain the importance of triangles in geometry.
questions.answer: Triangles are fundamental in geometry, used in constructions, proofs, and establishing properties of other polygons.

questions.question: Explain the importance of triangles in daily life.
questions.answer: Triangles are used in construction, engineering, bridges, and design due to their stability and structural strength.

questions.question: Explain the centroid, circumcenter, and orthocenter relation in an equilateral triangle.
questions.answer: In an equilateral triangle, the centroid, circumcenter, and orthocenter coincide at a single point.
